Explanation: The search results include a detailed history of the Mather Plaques, which are long-service awards associated with the National Park Service. The plaques are named after Stephen Tyng Mather, who was the first Director of the National Park Service and played a foundational role in its creation and ethos. This directly links the origin of the award to Mather, confirming that he is the person who first launched the award referenced in the question [78245].
